steps.title: Identify the relationship between the noun and examples
steps.content: The phrase “including the languages Tetum and Fijian” presents a noun followed directly by specific examples. The words “Tetum and Fijian” are integral to identifying which particular languages we're talking about—they specify and restrict the meaning of “the languages” rather than serving as a separate, parenthetical aside.

steps.title: Apply the rule for restrictive examples
steps.content: When examples immediately follow and restrict a noun in a simple phrase, no punctuation is required. This follows the same pattern as “the colors red and blue” or “the countries France and Germany.” A comma (A) or colon (B) would be appropriate if the examples were parenthetical or formally set apart, but here they're essential to the phrase. The em dash (D) is unnecessary. C correctly uses no punctuation, keeping “languages Tetum and Fijian” as a single, unified noun phrase. That matches choice C — C is correct.
